critic_raw:
Issue 1: Endpoint drift: LSTM on 24h sepsis, transformer on 12h vasopressor onset — non-comparable targets.

Issue 2: Encounter-level split leaks recurring ICU patients across training and holdout — inflates holdout AUROC.

Issue 3: Lab timestamp shift from charted to specimen collection time — feature availability window is not harmonized.

Issue 4: Cohort changes from individual admissions to merged episodes — different observational unit.
defender_raw: All four issues are conceded structurally. The benchmark was run under conditions that changed along with the model. The defense does not contest this. The argument for proceeding with a harmonized rerun rather than dismissing the transformer is: (1) the Hospital B external validation was prespecified and favorable; (2) the lactate and vasopressor features are pre-prediction-time and not target leakage; (3) the care-episode design has clinical merit. A clean rerun on shared conditions will determine whether the performance gap is real.
adjudication_raw:
Round 1: Critic raises 4 issues. Defender concedes all four at a structural level. 4 points resolved in Round 1. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

Round 2 (forced): No new substantive arguments from either side. Round 2 verdict identical to Round 1 with 0 new resolutions.

Final verdict: empirical_test_agreed. Hollow forced round: Round 2 verdict == Round 1 verdict AND Round 2 points_resolved == 0.
all_issues_raised: Issue 1: endpoint changed from 24h severe-sepsis to 12h vasopressor onset
Issue 2: encounter-level random split leaks recurring ICU patients across partitions
Issue 3: lab timestamp changed from EHR-charted to specimen collection time
Issue 4: cohort unit changed from individual admissions to merged cross-transfer episodes

empirical_test.condition: Rebuild shared cohort with one endpoint, one timestamp policy, one cohort construction rule, patient-level chronological split; rerun both models
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: AUROC edge falls below 0.01 and recall advantage disappears on harmonized cohorts
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Transformer leads by at least 0.02 AUROC and 5+ recall points at same alert rate on both hospitals
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Modest AUROC gain persists internally but recall advantage disappears, or gains inconsistent across hospitals
